channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Yolanda Saldívar thinks Selena 'partly to blame' for death: Report
item.description: Saldívar, 64, officially filed for parole in January. She shot Selena during a confrontation in a Texas hotel room.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Critics warn staff cuts at federal agencies overseeing US dams could put public safety at risk
item.description: Experts are warning that Trump administration workforce cuts at federal agencies overseeing U.S. dams are threatening their ability to provide reliable electricity, supply water to farmers and protect communities from floods.
item.dc:creator: Martha Bellisle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Big March storm fans wildfires and kills 3 as forecasters fear weekend tornado outbreak
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — A sprawling storm system crossing the U.S. on Friday caused deadly crashes, damaged buildings and fanned more than 100 wildfires in several central states, prompting evacuation orders in some communities.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY and RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Democrats confront limits of their minority power after bruising shutdown vote
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senate Democrats were grim Friday as they left Washington after a brutal 10-week stretch that consistently showed the limits of their power in the minority — and culminated with a deeply personal rupture over how to best counter President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Appeals court says Louisiana can carry out the state’s first nitrogen gas execution next week
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — Louisiana's first execution using nitrogen gas is set to move forward as planned next week after a federal appeals court on Friday vacated a preliminary injunction granted by a lower judge. With a March 18 date hastily nearing, attorneys for Jessie Hoffman Jr.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Doechii joins Will Smith to recreate viral TikTok trend
item.description: The trend uses her song "Anxiety" and spoofs a scene from "The Fresh Prince of Bell-Air" where Smith's character sneaks up on Ali's, who's dancing with her headphones on, and starts to mimic her.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, Fox 5

item.title: Oklahoma man says Social Security benefits terminated without warning or explanation
item.description: A local retiree says his Social Security benefits were suddenly suspended without warning — and with no explanation given when he reached out. He worries it may have to do with the place he was born, and ongoing DOGE cutbacks.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Humphrey/KFOR, Fox 5

item.title: Emerging from 2024 loss, Tim Walz holds a town hall in an Iowa Republican's district
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz stood before hundreds of Iowans on Friday and admitted he doesn't have all the answers about issues facing the country. “If I did, we wouldn't be in this goddamn mess,” Walz said.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 4 arrested in shooting death of 7-year-old Florida boy
item.description: "This is just the first round of arrests we will make in bringing Breon’s family the justice they deserve," the sheriff's office said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pledges to 'expose' his enemies in political speech at Justice Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump pledged to “expose” his enemies during a norm-breaking political speech Friday at the Justice Department in which he aired a litany of grievances about the criminal cases he faced and vowed retribution for what he described as the “lies and abuses that have o
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate votes to avoid government shutdown
item.description: The Senate voted mostly along party lines Friday afternoon to pass the House Republican-drafted bill to fund government through September, avoiding a government shutdown only hours before funding was due to lapse. President Trump is expected to sign the bill into law. The final vote was 54-46.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: More than 50 universities face federal investigations as part of Trump's anti-DEI campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than 50 universities are being investigated for alleged racial discrimination as part of President Donald Trump’s campaign to end diversity, equity and inclusion programs that his officials say exclude white and Asian American students.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Oklahoma Ethics Commission votes to pursue prosecution against State Supt. Walters
item.description: On Thursday, the Oklahoma Ethics Commission voted to pursue prosecution against State Superintendent Ryan Walters for alleged campaign violations.
item.dc:creator: Dylan Brown / KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: Viral 'American Idol' contestant dead at 32, family says
item.description: Douglas Kiker, the garbage collector who touched hearts with his singing voice on "American Idol" has died, his family announced on social media. He was 32 years old.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Helicopters permanently restricted on route near Washington airport where 67 died in midair crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Helicopters will be permanently restricted from flying near Washington, D.C.'s airport on the same route where a passenger jet and an Army helicopter collided in midair, killing 67 people, the Federal Aviation Administration said Friday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER and TARA COPP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cause of death for Gene Hackman and Betsy Arakawa's dog released
item.description: An examination of the dog found dead along with actor Gene Hackman and his wife in their Santa Fe home shows dehydration and starvation were likely what led to the animal’s demise.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Protestant denominations try new ideas as they face declines in members and money
item.description: When the Episcopal Church recently announced cuts to its national staff, it was the latest in a long-running cycle among historic U.S. Protestant denominations — declines in members leading to declines in funding and thus in staff. And it wasn't alone. The Presbyterian Church (U.S.A.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Voice of America ending contracts with Associated Press, Reuters and Agence France Presse
item.description: The government agency that runs the Voice of America has moved to terminate contracts with The Associated Press, Reuters and Agence France-Presse, and told its journalists Friday to stop using material from the wire services.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: USPS agrees to work with DOGE on reform, planning to cut 10,000 workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Postmaster General Louis DeJoy plans to cut 10,000 workers and billions of dollars from the U.S. Postal Service budget and he’ll do that working with Elon Musk ’s Department of Government Efficiency, according to a letter sent to members of Congress on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration moves to dismiss lawsuits against Iowa and Oklahoma over immigration laws
item.description: The Trump administration has moved to dismiss lawsuits against Iowa and Oklahoma for their immigration laws.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Fingerhut, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: International Delight coffee creamer shipped to 31 states is recalled
item.description: Danone, the distributor, issued a voluntary recall on some International Delight coffee creamers on Feb. 21 following reports of potential spoilage and illness related to the products' use.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Burn pit fund for veterans on chopping block in GOP spending bill
item.description: The fund helps former service members sickened by military toxic exposures, including burn pit smoke and Agent Orange contamination.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Congress poised to force $1B cut to local DC budget, surprising many lawmakers
item.description: Congress is poised to pass a President Trump-endorsed funding bill this week that Washington, D.C., officials warn would lead to a $1 billion cut to the District’s local budget, a move that has been catching members of both parties off guard.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: 12 taken to hospital with minor injuries after plane catches fire at Denver airport
item.description: Twelve people were taken to hospitals after an American Airlines plane landed at Denver International Airport on Thursday and caught fire, prompting slides to be deployed so passengers could evacuate quickly.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, Fox 5

item.title: 60 percent of voters unhappy with DOGE handling of federal workers: Poll
item.description: Elon Musk and DOGE have been trying to slash their way through the federal government, offering buyouts to and laying off scores of workers. Many of their actions are now the subject of lawsuits.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Luigi Mangione's defense fund raises over $722K
item.description: "People are raising money not for his innocence but because they're in support of allegedly what he did.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, KTLA 5

item.title: Once-routine immigration check-ins become high-stakes calculation as some are detained
item.description: When a couple from Colombia who was planning their wedding showed up for a check-in with U.S. immigration authorities, one was given his next appointment date. The other was detained and deported. Jhojan doesn’t know why Felipe was detained at the Feb.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, CEDAR ATTANASIO and SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Founding girl group member reveals she's been living in her car
item.description: "If you would have said to me while I was En Vogue, 'You're going to be living in your car one day,' I'd be like, 'Huh?"
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, Fox 5

item.title: VIDEO: American Airlines plane evacuated in Denver after fire aboard
item.description: Passengers were evacuated Thursday evening from an American Airlines flight after a fire was reported.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, ABC 4

item.title: 4-year-old calls 911 to say 'bad mom' ate his ice cream and needed to go to jail
item.description: We've all been there. You go to the freezer and discover your ice cream has been eaten, and not by you.
item.dc:creator: Laura Morrison, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Video shows missing college student's final moments before disappearance
item.description: Investigators have identified a person of interest in the disappearance of University of Pittsburgh student Sudiksha Konanki, who vanished while visiting the Dominican Republic on a spring break trip.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: EU retaliates as Trump steel, aluminum tariffs take effect
item.description: After announcing the response, the EU released a 99-page list of possible items that could be subject to tariffs, including fruit and vegetables, meat, alcoholic beverages, nicotine vapes and chewing gum.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Missing woman survives 6 days trapped in wrecked car as drivers pass unaware
item.description: For six days, Brieonna Cassell remained trapped inside her mangled Taurus as hundreds of cars passed by, unaware someone was in the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, ABC 4

item.title: Democrat David Gottfried easily wins Minnesota House special election, restoring a 67-67 power split
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Voters in a Minnesota House district at the center of post-election drama over chamber control decisively chose Democrat David Gottfried on Tuesday, tying the House and ending a short-lived Republican majority.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tiger Woods says he ruptured his Achilles tendon
item.description: It's yet another injury that would seem likely to keep him out of the Masters and perhaps other major championships this year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Why you'll no longer see 'excessive heat warnings' in the forecast
item.description: It may be hard to think about sweltering temperatures in early March but excessive heat watches and warnings are getting a new name.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: University of Pittsburgh student missing in Dominican Republic: What we know
item.description: The search involves joint efforts by authorities in the Dominican Republic, the United States and India.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, WGN 9
